req.title: Joint account operations (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-093 has three distinct testable behaviours: (1) a primary joint-account holder can successfully perform a transaction (positive / EP), (2) a secondary holder is forbidden from modifying ownership (negative / NEG + DT), and (3) the idempotency guarantee — submitting the exact same Idempotency-Key twice must return the original response without re-applying the operation (boundary / BVA, because the duplicate is the edge of the idempotency window). A fourth case covers the state transition from 'pending duplicate' back to 'already-applied' (STC). Together these four cases give full coverage of the stated requirement.

tests.name: test_primary_holder_transaction_succeeds
preconditions: Primary holder credentials available via <primary_auth_token fixture>
Account balance sufficient for the transaction amount
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with a valid transaction payload and a fresh Idempotency-Key
expected: Response body contains transaction id and status 'applied'
trace: R-FIN-093: 'a joint-account holder shall perform transactions'

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_ownership_change_forbidden
preconditions: Secondary holder credentials available via <secondary_auth_token fixture>
Secondary holder is NOT the primary/owning holder
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/joint with an ownership-change payload (e.g. new primary_holder_id)
expected: Response body contains error code indicating ownership modification is not permitted
trace: R-FIN-093: 'the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership'
R-FIN-093: 'error pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change'

tests.name: test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response
preconditions: Primary holder credentials available via <primary_auth_token fixture>
A specific Idempotency-Key has been used for exactly one prior successful request
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with transaction payload and Idempotency-Key K (first submission)
Record the first response body and status
POST /accounts/{id}/joint with identical payload and the same Idempotency-Key K (duplicate submission)
expected: Second HTTP response status equals first HTTP response status
Second response body transaction_id equals first response body transaction_id
Account balance is NOT debited a second time (operation applied only once)
trace: R-FIN-093: 'duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result'
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <api_client> import BankingApiClient

@pytest.fixture
def primary_client():
    return BankingApiClient(token=<primary_auth_token>)

def test_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_response(primary_client):
    account_id = <JOINT_ACCOUNT_ID>
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"type": "debit", "amount": 50, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    first = primary_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code in (200, 201)
    first_body = first.json()

    second = primary_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert second.status_code == first.status_code
    second_body = second.json()
    assert second_body.get("transaction_id") == first_body.get("transaction_id")
    assert second_body.get("status") == first_body.get("status")

tests.name: test_state_transition_pending_to_applied_on_resubmission
preconditions: Primary holder credentials available via <primary_auth_token fixture>
The API supports a 'pending' intermediate state observable before finalisation
A unique Idempotency-Key not previously submitted is available
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint to create a transaction that enters 'pending' state
POST /accounts/{id}/joint with same Idempotency-Key a second time after 'applied' state is reached
expected: First POST returns 200/201 with status 'pending' or 'applied'
Second POST with same key returns 200/201 with status 'applied' and same transaction_id (no new transaction created)
trace: STC: covers states pending -> applied and duplicate-key -> already-applied
tests.executable:
import uuid, time
import pytest
from <api_client> import BankingApiClient

@pytest.fixture
def primary_client():
    return BankingApiClient(token=<primary_auth_token>)

def test_state_transition_pending_to_applied_on_resubmission(primary_client):
    account_id = <JOINT_ACCOUNT_ID>
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"type": "debit", "amount": 25, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}

    first = primary_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code in (200, 201)
    txn_id = first.json()["transaction_id"]

    status = first.json().get("status")
    for _ in range(10):
        if status == "applied":
            break
        time.sleep(0.5)
        poll = primary_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint/transactions/{txn_id}")
        status = poll.json().get("status")
    assert status == "applied"

    second = primary_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/joint", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code in (200, 201)
    assert second.json()["transaction_id"] == txn_id
    assert second.json()["status"] == "applied"
